channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Vice Ganda shares humorous moment on It’s Showtime involving a 63-year-old admirer
item.description: The article highlights a light and entertaining segment from the popular noontime show “It’s Showtime,” featuring comedian and host Vice Ganda. In the segment, Vice Ganda jokingly mentioned or reacted...

item.title: Global Study Reveals Rapid Land Subsidence in Major River Deltas Threatening Millions
item.description: A comprehensive global study led by Leonard Ohenhen and Virginia Tech researchers has found that many of the world's largest river deltas are sinking faster than rising sea levels, putting over 236 mi...

item.title: John Ternus Appointed Apple CEO as Tim Cook Steps Down After 15 Years
item.description: After a remarkable 15-year tenure, Tim Cook will step down as Apple's CEO in September, with John Ternus, the company's long-serving hardware chief, taking the reins. Under Cook's leadership, Apple's ...

item.title: B.C. Government and First Nations to Co-Develop Approach to DRIPA
item.description: British Columbia Premier David Eby has announced that his government will collaborate directly with First Nations to develop a joint approach to the province's Declaration on the Rights of Indigenous ...

item.title: Nasarawa Deputy Speaker escapes unhurt in Keffi-Akwanga road accident
item.description: On Monday, the Deputy Speaker of the Nasarawa State House of Assembly, Rt. Hon. Muhammad Adamu Oyanki, waka enter road crash along the Keffi-Akwanga highway around 10 a.m. His car suffer damage but fo...

item.title: Pakistan High Commission appoints Gerry's Visa Services to assist overseas Pakistanis in UK
item.description: The Pakistan High Commission in London has officially confirmed that it awarded a contract to Gerry’s Visa Services Ltd to provide extra consular services throughout the United Kingdom. This decision,...

item.title: Cocaine in Wastewater Affects Salmon Movement and Behaviour
item.description: Cocaine contamination in aquatic environments is becoming a growing concern for wildlife. A recent study tracked the movement of juvenile Atlantic salmon in Lake Vättern, Sweden, over an eight-week pe...

item.title: Mamata Banerjee accuses BJP of deliberately delaying her flight by planting officers at the airport
item.description: Mamata Banerjee, the Chief Minister of West Bengal, has accused the Bharatiya Janata Party (BJP) of deliberately delaying her flight by 'cherry-picking' officers at the airport. According to her, her ...

item.title: Victor Wembanyama makes NBA history as first unanimous Defensive Player of the Year
item.description: Victor Wembanyama has set a historic milestone by becoming the first player in NBA history to earn a unanimous Defensive Player of the Year (DPOY) award. The San Antonio Spurs center was voted first o...

item.title: How to Recognise and Avoid AI-Driven Recruitment Scams
item.description: Recruitment scams exploiting AI are on the rise, targeting jobseekers with fake offers to steal money or personal information. Fraudsters create convincing emails and job descriptions tailored to an i...

item.title: Mzansi Threaded redefines women’s fanwear ahead of the 2026 World Cup
item.description: A fresh trend in South African fan culture is taking shape as women embrace more stylish and expressive ways to support their teams. Dubbed the “Go Sports” girl movement, this shift blends national pr...

item.title: Controversy at Houghton Golf Club Over Ban on Political Flags
item.description: Houghton Golf Club in Johannesburg is under fire after reprimanding member Louis Seeco for displaying a Palestinian flag on his vehicle. Seeco stated on social media that he was approached by the club...

item.title: Why Informal Markets Dominate Trade in Africa and What Businesses Must Learn
item.description: This article reviews a book by Farayi Ziswa which explains how informal markets across Africa operate and why they continue to dominate trade. The writer reflects on personal experience in Dar es Sala...

item.title: Key terms outlined in Bell Canada’s AI data centre agreement with RM of Sherwood
item.description: A newly detailed agreement between Bell Canada and the Rural Municipality (RM) of Sherwood outlines the terms for developing a major artificial intelligence (AI) data centre in the Regina area. The de...

item.title: Former Foreign Office official to explain decision in Mandelson vetting dispute
item.description: Sir Olly Robbins, the former Permanent Under Secretary at the Foreign Office, is set to give evidence to Parliament following his dismissal over a dispute concerning the vetting of Lord Peter Mandelso...

item.title: Police Officer Critically Injured During Protest at Manipur Block Development Office
item.description: In Manipur’s Wangjing area, tensions remain high following ethnic violence that has affected multiple communities. On Monday night, hundreds of protesters assembled at a newly constructed Block Develo...

item.title: Boda Boda Riders in Tharaka Nithi Unite to Support Sick Boy with KSh 165k
item.description: In a heartwarming display of community spirit, boda boda riders in Tharaka Nithi, Kenya, rallied together to support the family of young Musa Kihara, who requires KSh 2 million for medical treatment. ...

item.title: Suspect remanded to judicial custody after bomb threat to US Consulate in Karachi
item.description: A judicial magistrate in Karachi has sent a suspect, identified as Sharjeel Akbar, to prison on judicial remand in connection with a case involving threats to bomb the US Consulate General. The accuse...

item.title: JI approaches SHC seeking contempt action against Nepra for ignoring orders on Karachi loadshedding
item.description: Jamaat-i-Islami (JI) has filed a contempt of court application in the Sindh High Court (SHC) against officials of the National Electric Power Regulatory Authority (Nepra), accusing them of failing to ...

item.title: Addressing systemic barriers to local medical technology innovation in South Africa
item.description: South Africa’s medical technology sector suffers from heavy import dependence, with around 90% of devices sourced from abroad and the government as the main buyer. This reliance mirrors challenges in ...
